examples.title: ArrayList Operations Demo
examples.description: Demonstrates common ArrayList operations including add, remove, iterate
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the main ways to work with ArrayList: adding elements at specific positions, removing by object or index, and various iteration techniques. The key point is using Iterator's remove() method rather than collection's remove() during iteration to avoid ConcurrentModificationException.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for iteration, O(n) for add at specific index (shifting), O(1) for add at end
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements, plus O(capacity) for internal array

examples.title: Custom Sorting with Comparator
examples.description: Shows how to sort collections using custom comparators for multiple sorting strategies
examples.explanation: This demonstrates Comparator's power to define multiple sorting strategies without modifying the Employee class. The thenComparing() method allows multi-level sorting. Method references (Employee::getName) provide clean, readable comparator creation.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing elements, O(log n) for sorting auxiliary space

examples.title: HashMap Operations and Iteration
examples.description: Comprehensive HashMap usage including various iteration patterns
examples.explanation: HashMap provides rich APIs beyond basic put/get. compute(), merge(), and computeIfAbsent() are powerful methods for atomic updates. The stream-based iteration allows functional-style processing with filtering and mapping.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average for put/get/contains, O(n) for iteration
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing n key-value pairs
